text.full: Beautiful restaurant. A bit too stale. I'll review the pastries and their pizza. Pastries are not all that. Macaroons are just ok. It's mostly all hype due to its perfect location and their cool trendy patrons. Restaurant is clean and fancy and all white making it seem super sterile. They want to make NY style in LA and it doesn't work but most aren't apt to understand. Service is rushed. I like asking questions to somewhat test the employees. Big fail. Employee was not able to tell me what flour is used and if their pastry had nuts.

Beautiful location, wonderful service, delicious food~ what more can you ask for?
Had their Caesar salad (yum) and pepperoni pizza-
The dough was amazing--- crispy yet chewy and the sauce was wonderful (not too acidic)-

The interior is open and airy and very spacious for a downtown joint

Still underwhelmed. Fairly empty when we came for weekend brunch. Their best dish was their portobello fries with aioli. Otherwise, their waffles, benedicts, and egg sandwich were just mediocre and overpriced.
Absolutely wonderful experience from operator, to manager, to server, to busboy, to counter salesman, to coffee bar, to valet. And the food was delicious as well.
I really enjoy the pastel dishes, salads, and pizza. The atmosphere and service are wonderful too!
Good food great aesthetics! The food was really good and the ambience was amazing if you're visiting Los Angeles this is great for a good dining experience plus some fresh baked goods.

Loveeee this restaurant! It's my GO TO! Brought my girls here last night and shoutout to Rafael our waiter and the team. They were so apologetic for no reason ! bc they brought out my friends dish before ours and let her eat for free and then gave us free dessert!! Love the service, food and ambiance!
This French restaurant/bakery is absolutely stunning, classy and absolutely delicious food. The bakery is off the chain. This place is five stars all the way around.

I've had brunch and dinner here and my absolute favorite item to get is the tartufo pizza. It is definitely a bougie pizza costing you $50. But, you do get generous amount of truffle. As soon as the pizza arrives to your table, you can just smell the truffle. 

The salads always taste fresh and pasta very chewy. 
The staff is super professional and friendly. 

One of my favorite restaurants to take out-of-town friends. They're always impressed by the hand towels and fresh flowers in the bathroom :D

After attending an amazing evening concert at the Crypto Arena...


Followed by a night full of shaking what my momma gave me at a local Latin club in DTLA...


Then finally hitting the pillow in the early hours of the morning and sleeping like an absolute baby at the coolest Downtown Boutique Hotel...



YOU KNOW for a fact that as much as my wife & I missed our three kids who we'd left overnight with their Auntie...


  We were just not quite ready to return to reality. 


So on the way home the next morning before making our way to the 101 fwy...


We decided Sunday Brunch was in order and what better place to brunch it up then....



    [REDACTED] in Downtown Los Angeles!!!!



Wait? What?? 


          You've never heard of [REDACTED]?? 



                       Well, you my friend....

              
                         Are. In. For. A. Treat. 



        Hey, if anyone knows how to Brunch...


                             It's. The. French. 

                        

And I don't know about you...

But I'm pretty sure the French Revolution was really started over Brunch and not over Social & Political upheaval like the history books claim ;)


So yeah, for those of you whom haven't been to B.L. Yet, let me tell ya....


From the moment you walk in, you are instantly transformed into another world. 


From the World Class Baked Goods....

Just wait until you see all of the goodies behind their glass case lines. 

(From their Beignets and Croissants to their Fruit Tarts and colorful Macarons) if you have a sweet tooth...


            You. Will. Definitely. Be. In. Trouble. 


              And that's JUST THE BEGINNING!!



If you happen to make it past all the baked goods, and stay for Sunday Brunch, be prepared to have your mind FREAKIN' blown! 


My wife went with their Le Pancake (a Soufflé-Style Pancake with Chantilly Cream & Strawberry Sauce) 


              -----Sounds Fancy doesn't it-----


and her eyes nearly rolled into the back of her head, I thought she was gonna have a... 


                           oh never mind =) 


As for me...

Mine was good, but apparently not that good ;) 


I had their Croque Madame Sandwich (Jambon de Paris, Melted Gruyère, Dijon & Béchamel with a sunny side egg on sliced pain de mie) accompanied by THE cutest arugula salad ever...


Let's just say....


         I was ready to meet my maker that day!


                 Yeah, it was that dang GOOD! 


We did share their $10 glass of Fresh Squeezed O.J. (TOTALLY WORTH IT) by the way...


(We were in awe about the family next to us who had about $50 bucks worth of O.J. at their table)...

                              Ballers!!! LOL


And of course we each had a beautiful Cappuccino to start our morning off RIGHT! 


Other Brunch Specialties that caught my eye and are definitely on my radar for our next Brunch outing here are....


-The Lobster Hash ($35) 

-The 16oz bone in Ribeye with Eggs ($65!!!) 

-The Tartufo Pizza (Mozzarella, Fontina, truffle pecorino, crème fraîche, black truffle mushroom pâté, soft cooked egg & shaved Sabatino black truffles) ($50) 


So, who's coming with me and which one are you getting?? 


I know you ladies are all getting the 'Le Pancake' 

                                      LOL 


Of course Lunch & Dinner are also Top of The Class
and you really can't go wrong with anything this place has. 

This DTLA location is their original spot, but they also have a location which we've stopped by once before for some Macarons to go in WEHO (West Hollywood) which BTW has THE FANCIEST restroom that I have EVER seen in my ENTIRE LIFE!! 


So if you find yourself in Los Angeles...


Visiting, on business, or just for pleasure....


Do yourself a solid and come to [REDACTED] and 


                    GET one of EVERYTHING!!!

businessPhotos.caption: There's Tiffany Blue and then there's Bottega Louie PINK!
One more guest would NOT Be Good!
The infamous Le Pancake that will make you.... (Read my review for the answer)
Meet Croque Madame! She will make your day!

text.full: Love this place but it's always busy busy and long waiting time, I drove all the way from OC to there and they didn't except reservations only walk in, then walking time time was 90 minutes, love the food and pastries but not the walking period.

I came here for dinner on Valentine's Day and service was amazing. At first I wasn't able to find valet so I had to park elsewhere but later I did find it so next time I will use valet. The wait was not long at all even though I'm sure it was a busy night for them. Food and drinks were amazing. I got the gnocchi and husband got the margarita  pizza and we got two alcoholic drinks the green apple and unfortunately I can't recall the other drink but both were delicious. Dessert was also delicious we got the chocolate raspberry cake and the beignet but amazing and perfect. Will definitely be coming back.

I don't know what made the restaurant better, either the exceptional customer service, or the food.

Both are really great!

I came here for my birthday with family and friends.

It is located in the financial district of Dtla.

I love the look of the restaurant.

I like the fact that you can see the chef's preparing your food - and let's not forget about the beautiful, great tasting food and desserts.

My waiter was the best!

He made my birthday memorable here! Iwill definitely book another birthday party here next year!

businessPhotos.caption: My bf and I at Bottega Louie's for my birthday party..
Birthday cake made of red velvet,mousse and cream..plate adorned with macarons..and a happy birthday sign made out of chocolate.

text.full: The food was absolutely delicious, we had the smoked salmon and the smoked salmon Benedict. Flavors were great and it tasted fresh. The only downside was our water had a floaty (looked like a chili flake) in the bottle, when we asked for a different one, the second one was brought with another floaty. Most likely from the closing team not properly washing/polishing at the end of night to prep for the morning team. Other than that, service was great and our waiter (don't remember name - sorry:( ) was phenomenal and helpful!

Love [REDACTED] one of the best restaurants in la area
Parking : very difficult to find street parking pay need to park in near by paid parking $12-15 
Atmosphere : upstairs casual dining very clean and modern
Service : top notch service . Servers are extremely nice and friendly 
Food: 10/10 must order are the portobello fries and [REDACTED] salad and pizza 

If you are in the area it's a must try . I been going there for many years before it became popular with celebrities.

This is one of my go-to DTLA spots, especially for lunch. I've been for dinner a handful of times, but I usually come for lunch especially when I have friends visiting from out of town. It's always a solid choice.

The service is consistently good, though it can be a bit slow when they're busy. I've always been a little curious about how they decide which tables receive the complimentary bread? On my last visit, every table around me got hot bread except ours but the restaurant was packed and our server was the only one covering our section, so I imagine it was just a timing issue.

The pizza and espresso martinis are my favorites. The macarons and berry tarts are a very close second.

Had the most beautiful Valentine's Day lunch at [REDACTED]. The space is stunning in the daytime -- bright, airy, and effortlessly romantic.

I ordered the salmon plate and it was perfectly cooked -- tender, flavorful, and beautifully presented. To drink, I had the À La Passion and the green apple Paloma -- both fresh, vibrant, and so refreshing.

Such an elevated, indulgent spot for a Valentine's celebration. Definitely one of my favorite luxe lunch experiences in LA.

Service took forever and the food was very flavorless. The bread was cold and hard it almost broke my last three teeth i have left. The only good things were the mac n cheese and the water. its super loud inside and they put u super close to other people like sardines. Do not recommend STAY AWAY. BEWARE

author.displayLocation: Van Nuys, San Fernando Valley, CA

text.full: very disappointed that I was charged 5% FEE FOR A SERVICE CHARGE. after paying a significant amount of money for breakfast. $55 for breakfast and then a 5% service charge for a back   something like that.

Visited last week for a friends birthday. Service was very slow despite the restaurant being nearly empty. The downtown location is quite beautiful and elegant with its grand ceilings and accents. The pastries on display were all beautiful and beautifully expensive. I ordered an espresso martini, which was alright, it tasted watered down. My table shared the arancini which was delicious, and the tomato bruschetta. I wish the bruschetta came with buffala mozzarella cheese and fresh onion to complete, because it was a bit boring with just tomatoes. For entree, we shared a margherita pizza and cacio e pepe pasta. Pasta was just okay, very small portioned cooked al dente, and it needed more cheese/sauce. The pizza was pretty good, light and crisp. It was nice to visit one time, but I most likely will not return. My table felt very dismissed from the servers. Took about 25 minutes to receive check and to go boxes.
Food was amazing and staff was super nice and friendly I love this place and will be going back!!
Another amazing brunch experience here! Eggs Benedict was fantastic, service from Napoleon who was so friendly and attentive. Had to have a pear martini also! Soooo perfect every, single, time.

Whether you're here for a meal, a drink at the cafe bar area, or to check out the beautiful pastries section. This place will leave your mouth watering. I've been quite a few times as well as the West Hollywood location. Both great and completely different from each other. Downtown location has that open high ceiling New York vibe. A beautiful pastries section right when you walk in with all the artful creation in the glass display. Plus the famous macarons all lined up.

West Hollywood, a smaller location but it does have patio seating in front as well as covered patio out back. More lush garden vibe. And their pastries and bar area is right in the middle of the restaurant. Both beautifully decorated which gives it that extra touch. Didn't have a meal this time but desserts were picked up at the downtown location for a special night. My bday of course...Lol... If you haven't had any of their pastries, it's a must. Each items are well decorated and beautifully made that you don't want to eat them. And their famous macarons, bite size goodies. They are a little on the pricey side but worth it. Cakes are amazing which is great for gathering or a hostess gift. We had the fruit tart which is somewhat seasonal as during the summer it wasn't available the last time. It was replaced with a peach tart. Still amazing though. I assume as it was a seasonal fruit, it was brought out for the summer.

Fruit tart is alway a top choice for us as it's pretty simple but oh so good. A nice crunchy base but flakey. Filled with a delicious custard and topped with amazing berries. A slice is great but a whole cake is better. Regardless whatever you choose, there's never a wrong thing. Only thing that sucks, they can't write anything for you. If it's a birthday, they'll add a chocolate bday sign instead. 

Parking is a stretch at this location. Valet or a parking structure. Street parking is a bitch downtown. But you can't go wrong with [REDACTED]....
Food was so good and authentic!  The place was nice and the staff was friendly and welcoming

author.displayLocation: Canoga Park, San Fernando Valley, CA

text.full:
I went to an early dinner at [REDACTED] in Downtown Los Angeles, and it was my first time visiting with a friend, so I really didn't know what to expect.

They offer a variety of menu options -- sandwiches, pastas, pizzas, and salads -- but since they're known for their pizza, we decided to split one.

We ordered the Margherita pizza and asked if we could add a few vegetables. We originally wanted bell peppers, but they didn't have those available as an add-on. Instead, we added olives and a vegetable topping that was similar to broccoli.

The crust was thin, which some people may really like. My friend enjoyed it more than I did -- for me, it was just okay. I think I was expecting a little more flavor or richness. It almost tasted a bit "too healthy" for what I usually want from a pizza -- LOL. I did have a few slices, but if I go back, I'd probably try a different style of pizza.

One thing that did surprise us was the bill -- they added $5 for the veggie add-ons plus a surcharge. We were a little shocked by that, but when we asked about it, they did remove the charge, which we appreciated.

Overall, the restaurant itself is beautiful. The space is elegant, open, and very aesthetically pleasing. Even the bathrooms were stunning -- decorated in gold and white and very pretty.

Our waitress was very nice and attentive, which made the experience even better.

They also have a front dessert counter filled with beautiful pastries and desserts that looked amazing. I didn't try any this time, but I would definitely love to come back and sample a few on my next visit.

I would definitely go back again -- but next time, I'd try a different pizza or maybe explore some of their other menu items (and dessert!).
Lovely food and ambiance. You can never go wrong with [REDACTED]! Every time I've been there, I've been satisfied.
We love the food, drinks, pastries & the service. We are repeat customers & will go back.

Came here for lunch, it's been awhile since I've revisited, glad I did. I had the black truffle arancini, Caesar and a latte.. the arancini's sauce was every damn thing  the Caesar was well dressed and I love the crunchy croutons on top... definitely a winner. I was full by the time the arancini arrived but oh my  everything was tasting great I'm leaving the review before I even payed for my food  the truffle flavor was light but definitely throughout the dish, my server was attentive and explained the menu to me with much fervor. I hope to try the pizza the young man at the station looked like he was locked in over there  I love a place where it's lively and people sound like they are enjoying the combos and food. I especially enjoy the big windows, and can't wait to hit the dessert side, the grand macaron looks epic. Will definitely return.

Pretty disappointing experience. Still sitting here. They have a Dine LA option but they are "out of most options" but don't tell you until after you order and the first course arrives. Literally the Patisserie is out of all three options. Including an affogato. Really out of vanilla ice cream and espresso. Hmm. Seems suspect to me. We won't be back

Went here for my birthday and so happy I came here. Food was really good and loved the bread they give you at the beginning. Tuna tartare tasted really fresh, though I wish there were a little more tuna and less tomatoes. The salad I picked had crab in it, I wish there were more crab in the salad. Servers were really friendly. Place was really lively. Would definitely come back to try the other items on the menu. I didn't get to try the desserts yet, but they look really amazing. I'll have to come back and try it!

Is this what West Hollywood is about? It's one of my friend's favorite bakeries in the area and when I came to visit from out of town, she wanted to take me there. We opted to grab some sweet treats and take them home. 

I decided to treat myself to their six piece macaron box as a small birthday treat. The guy who helped me must have been the manager because he was dressed in a full on tuxedo. He asked me which flavors I wanted and I told him just to surprise me and to mix it up. He stated that he will just give me their most popular flavors; GREAT.

When I got home, I realized he only gave me 4 flavors; 2 salted caramel, 1 strawberry, 2 raspberry, and 1 mango.

I highly doubt that these were the most popular flavors. How lazy did you have to be to give me repeats and to only reach for all the ones in the same box/area. The whole display was filled with macaroni of every flavor. I could see if they were running out. But honestly he was just being lazy.

The only thing he did well was the wrapping of the box with the bow.

Strolling by , been wanting to visit this place for YEARS !


Wanted to take my lil one for a coffee date and so i did

Service 4/5 polite , prompt , responsive, and well mannered 

This place has an alore and a general type of crowd , a lot of business casual , nose jobs , and. " high class " but a lot fake  funk , so don't be intimidated to come , I've been dinning in every rooftop in Los angles and never felt out of place for some reason i avoided this place because i thought it was out of my league 

But the the staff is well trained and polite also efficient, so i def will down here for the full experience 

Flat white 4/5
Macroons 5/5
Service 5/5
Walking distance 5/5

5 macroons and a flat white 12oz will run you about 24$ keep that in mind, i don't think
Expensive but some
Might

Food: 5*
Service: 4*
Ambiance: 5*

With some colleagues, we shared the calabrese pizza, arugula salad, grilled broccolini, and the white truffle arancini.

Calabrese pizza
Large, ample toppings, and the pickled Fresno chilies were a nice touch. The pizza was delicious.

Arugula salad
As you would expect. Well seasoned and tasty. It added freshness to the meal.

broccolini 
The broccolini was tender crisp. Perfectly cooked and tasty.

Arancini 
It was good. White truffle always hits right. The arancini insides were nice and hot. All in all a comforting treat.

The service was good, but not great. They weren't that busy, not bad, just not memorable for being welcoming or exceptional.

The ambiance was nice. Upscale, yet cozy.

Talk about personalized service! Pablo is absolutely incredible. A very kind individual and he treated us great! Food is delicious, coffee is immaculate, and the pastries are top tier! A very cozy ambiance. I highly recommend!

Fantastic experience all around! The food was delicious, fresh, and beautifully prepared. The atmosphere was welcoming, and the service was friendly and attentive. Everything exceeded our expectations--we'll definitely be coming back.

The bite-sized macarons were displayed by color, making me want to try every flavor. I ordered chocolate, strawberry, pistachio, and vanilla, and I loved how the flavors were rich yet clean. The raspberry cream doughnut was also delicious; it wasn't too sweet, and the cream filling was perfectly balanced. Noise level was higher than others.

The [REDACTED] salad is a namesake of the place. The salad was a relic from the past. Very 80's with thousand island dressing, but modernized with the cut up lettuce. We added an extra chicken breast to boost the protein. The burrata pizza was delicious. The crust is light and airy with the right amount of crunch. 

A must order is the chocolate soufflé. It was light and just sweet enough to curb that sugar fix. One of the best desserts that I've had in a while. 

The place is huge, but feels stark with the white walls. They call it minimalist, I guess. Service was excellent. My suggestion would be to make a reservation so you don't need to wait. He was attentive and gave great advice on the food. Super knowledgeable and smart. 

The pastries look delicious, almost too good to eat. Perhaps next time when I'm not stuffed, I'll grab one. 

It isn't cheap with almost $100 for the entire meal, but the quality is in the details!

businessPhotos.caption: Louie salad with chicken breast on the side

text.full:
I came here with a group of friends for someone's birthday and it was an excellent experience. I ordered the carbonara and was impressed by the quality of the noodles and of the soft boiled egg. Overall, it was a really good carbonara, and something I would order again. Everyone else I was with also very complimentary of their food. Service was quick and attentive. Overall, I was pleasantly surprised by the dinner. 

At the front of the store there is a large dessert display and a coffee counter. Their macaroons and cakes are across the board.

Was walking by with my friends and immediately had to make a pit stop. 

There's a display of macarons in various flavors. also a display case of many other desserts to choose from. 

There is also a restaurant and bar attached so you can have a full meal, but I was happily with my small dessert. 

It was about $20 for a box of 6. The line moved quickly, so you better figure out what flavors you want!
Pablo and Gavin are AMAZING! Their service was great and the drinks/pizza were better

We just stopped in to get some macarons. They are beautiful and have a wide selection of flavors like birthday cake, earl grey, chocolate, and many more. They also have coffee/tea and giant croissants and pain au chocolat. 

The macarons are really nothing special, especially considering the price, but their drinks are very tasty. We got the cinnamint latte and it was delicious. My mom got the macchiato bread pudding which she said "tasted like a bran muffin" so she was kinda disappointed. 

I have been here for dinner once, it was around 10 years ago, and i do remember the service and food being wonderful. A very nice time. I can see people trying it for the experience. For me personally, while my husband and i had a great time, i havent been back to dine in those 10 years, so that should tell you something.

Vic provided superb service. If an Oscar's for bartender existed, Vic would be first runner up. He has mastered a tasteful and elegant craft catered to his guest. Drinks were tasteful and aesthetically pleasing. Ambience emaculate. Food O* to your mouth!
Amazing!  Great food. Great service. Pretty good location. Neat place. Highly recommended.

Went on a Saturday night with a reservation and was seated immediately. Place was packed but service was good and attentive. Good place for any number of different parties. 

Went for the pasta/fries/meat pizza/Brussels sprouts. Everything was really delicious. Didn't have any complaints. I would say the brussels sprouts were a bit too citrusy. Pizza and the pasta were really good. Cooked to perfection and really enjoyed my time here. Had low expectations but everything was everything pretty decent. 

Parking as a pain, but there is valet and street parking. Uber is probably the best option if you're close in the area, which was my choice of transportation.
The trenne pasta is top tier here. Can't find crispy pasta like this anywhere else. The cakes and pastries never let me down.

My wife and I went for brunch on our Anniversary and loved it! The Lobster Hash and Spinach and Goat Cheese Omelette were excellent. After brunch, we stopped by the cafe and picked up a Macaron and a Beignet. 10+. We will be taking the family next time. The service was good, and the ambiance was great. We loved the open kitchen concept.
Our visit here has been very pleasant. The background was serene and welcoming the cuisine was tasty and the staff were considered but not intrusive
Really enjoyed this coffee shop. Excellent coffee, fresh pastries, and a warm, inviting atmosphere. Great service and attention to detail.

Place was delicious, everything we ate was a burst of flavor. The service was great and super kind with great recommendations. The restaurant was super nice we sat in the back outside area and it was beautiful!

[REDACTED]'s bakery is one of DTLA's most irresistible stops -- a bright, bustling patisserie filled with beautiful pastries and colorful macarons that taste just as good as they look. The cases are packed with fruit tarts, celebration cakes, and French‑style desserts that feel special without being fussy, and everything is presented with that signature [REDACTED] polish. It's definitely on the pricier side, but the quality, freshness, and gorgeous presentation make it worth the splurge, whether you're grabbing a gift box or treating yourself to something sweet after brunch.

Deliciously immaculate. Americano is very smoothly brewed. You can think of this place as 3-in-1 cafe, bar, restaurant. 

- Parking -
There's an LAZ public parking right across the street! 

Valet is $15 :(
Great ambiance. The food was pretty delicious....and of course their macaroons were on point. Ordered a birthday surprise to which they brought out delicious, fresh and creamy Pistachio ice cream.

I am definitely a fan of [REDACTED]. 

I was only ever familiar with their desserts and pastries. However, that's changed since moving to the area. Their cocktail bar is phenomenal, the bartenders are experts at their craft and their selection of top shelf spirits is truly note worthy.

Our first dinner experience here we noticed everyone ordering pizza. So we went with the general consensus as there had to be something to this and boy, their pizza is delicious. I would never had guessed that for [REDACTED] but here we are. 

Definitely still going back for the pastries but in addition [REDACTED] offers an upscale cocktail experience along with beautifully made pizzas right down stairs. Highly recommended.

I really enjoyed dining at [REDACTED]. The service was great, as well as the food. We had ordered the tuna tartare, meatballs, and the salmon. All the food came out at a reasonable time. The ambiance was very historic and charming. Parking is a bit of a nightmare, so best to just valet.

Love, Love [REDACTED].

Excellent location. 

Beautiful restaurant. 

Great service. Great cocktails. Great pasta. 

Not a lot of parking choices but it's downtown L. A. So it's kinda' expected. 

I look forward to coming back soon. 

Happy. New. Year!

First place that I ate on my stop in LA for the rose bowl and it definitely had its highs and lows. As someone who came from DC for this I had already eaten breakfast but they only allowed me to eat from the breakfast menu while ordering. That was my only critique as I was looking for some lunch, now onto the good parts. The service was amazing with my waiter consistently checking in on me making sure my water was filled, my food was up to par, or if I needed anything else. The atmosphere of the place is also something that was really special you start off by walking into seeing all of the candy and macaroons and keep pushing through to see the awesome restaurant design.  All in all it was a great place to eat.

Hmm...it's a little hit and miss.  Cacio de Pepe was good but the pizza was bad - soggy and weird aftertaste.  The Mac and cheese was runny and needed salt. The gelato was good but by 9pm they were pretty much out of everything else.

Exquisite food. Exquisite service. This place never fails to impress. Even when I've come here before moving to LA, being out of town, I chose this place to meet up with Angelenos for business meetings - and they were impressed. It really is that special.

They're also accommodating for large groups. In fact, I don't think I've ever been here with fewer than seven people, and it's always handled smoothly. Visitors inevitably gravitate toward the candy counter on the way out -- it's hard not to. The entire space is incredibly aesthetically pleasing, from the marble to the displays.

One note: with so much marble, the acoustics can make conversation a bit challenging in certain areas of the restaurant.

This is a popular bakery and dessert shop, so its desserts and pastries are its most delicious and famous items. The Napoleon cake is the best I've ever had; it's sweet but not cloying, and even those who don't have a big sweet tooth should try it. The macarons are also a specialty, with many colors and exquisite designs. As for the other dishes, it depends on personal preference. The ribs are very tender and delicious, but be aware that the pizzas may contain goat cheese. The prices are reasonable, and the service is good.这是一家网红面包甜点店，所以最好吃也最出名的是他家的甜点和糕点，其中，拿破仑是我吃过最好的拿破仑，甜而不腻，即便不是那么喜欢甜食的人都值得一试，马卡龙也是他家的特色，颜色很多，样式精致。菜品则看个人喜好，肋排很嫩而且美味，披萨可能都带羊奶酪需要注意。价格公道，服务好。

This is just a review on their pannetone. But I just had to write one because it is SO BAD! It's one of the worst and driest pannetone I've ever had. The bread doesn't taste good. It's just meh. All the fruit inside is so sparsely populated and only found outside of the bread. The fruit they picked is so sad, too (just raisins?).  I don't know if anyone at the bakery knows what pannetone is supposed to taste like? I decided to eat some with tea on Christmas Eve but this pannetone is so shockingly bad, I don't know what to do with the rest. People have warned me not to eat at here because it's one of those restaurants that's all decor and no substance so I haven't. I think I won't, after this experience with the pannetone.

Still firing on all cylinders despite being the middle of office party holiday season. Extremely great service and attentive hosting. Came at 11am and immediately ready for meals at 11:15am. Party of 7. Took Metro over with an easy stroll from the West exit (Hope St) at 7th & Metro. So superior to other Italian offerings in DTLA. Always a great pleasure dining here (though based upon the relatively open table layout - this is a former department store location with high ceilings, I'm not sure it's great for romantic dining as it can be a bit noisy, but the wine and bar selections are excellent, including non-alcoholic options.)

businessPhotos.caption: Espresso eclair, Yuzu and calamansi eclair, Mango lime napoleon
King Salmon, Tagliatelle Bolognese,  Fettuccine Belmondo
Lobster bisque soup and Portobello Fries
Italian salad with side of Burrata & Roasted Vine Tomatoes

text.full: I love going there for the macarons! They are delicious and have other amazing deserts. The service was great , food and drinks could have been better. No flavor on the breakfast potatoes, but the pizza was really good and fresh.

I got sick from the lobster hash.  Not violently ill, but enough to question the food prep/ handling.  
My companions' pizza and smoked salmon Benedict were fine though.

I was extremely disappointed the food as usual was terrific. The service was terrible. I sat for 15 to 20 minutes before anyone paid attention to me.My waiter was rude. Despite my huge tip, I was treated terribly. I will come again only because I love the food and previously.service sucked food was great

Service was amazing but the food had pros and cons. The goat cheese omelette seemed like a no brainer but was not as expected, not enough cheese filling for my liking. The sweet breakfast items were definitely good. Wish I would tried the pizza but had already ordered enough food. Maybe next visit.

If you go to the macaron counter the lady who's been working there for quite a while now needs to learn how to greet and be friendly to customers. Whenever I go and buy macarons cakes etc she's always with a long face  I don't think that's a good attitude for a place like [REDACTED]. Other than that I always like the food here. The lady from the restaurant was nice and accommodating.

author.displayLocation: Wilshire Center - Koreatown, Los Angeles, CA

text.full: Always amazing. One of my favorite brunch spots. Great food and service. Never imagined having a breakfast pasta but it was amazing.

This place is an experience. If you love desserts, they have tons of special Macarons that people travel long distance for.

I love to sit at the bar and drink mimosas for brunch. They do get pretty busy though, so be warned you may wait.

Also worth noting they have never given us trouble over bringing our small dog. They've been very welcoming.

Be prepared to pay a premium price for small-ish plates on the brunch menu. I cannot speak to the other menus as I've only dined here for brunch.

The atmosphere is great. It's a grand space with a lot of tables to accommodate your party. DTLA staple.

I rarely write reviews, but [REDACTED] in LA left me no choice. Where do I even start? The management and host could teach a masterclass in rudeness--apparently courtesy is optional here. And if you have leftovers you wanna take home? They don't box the food for you--they just hand you a box with your leftovers and let you figure it out yourself. And the bread... oh, the bread... stale, sad, and frankly offensive to carbs everywhere. Service was painfully slow and disinterested, like they were doing us a favor by showing up at all. I love Italian food, but [REDACTED] made me question why I even bother leaving my house. If you want charm, warmth, and food that actually travels well, keep walking.

Swanky joint if you're looking to make an impression. Italian food is fresh hot but be ready to wait. Quality takes time! My suggestion is that they at minimum serve complimentary bread for the table if there's a 20 minute wait or longer for food. They were glad to get life at this request but it's nerving to have to ask. Good spot for brunch if you're feeling fancy! We also got some yummy macaroons on the way out.

[REDACTED] - DTLA

[REDACTED] never misses. From the moment you walk in, the ambiance is unreal - bright, lively, and framed by those gorgeous high ceilings that make the whole space feel grand. The open kitchen adds to the vibe, letting you watch the action while you wait (and drool a little).

The pastries are absolutely elite (the macarons are dangerous), the brunch menu is always on point, and the staff is consistently great and friendly, even when the place is slammed.

It's one of those spots where you can take anyone - friends, coworkers, out-of-towners -- and everyone leaves impressed. Beautiful space, great food, and top-tier vibes. A DTLA staple for a reason.

It IS very loud due to the wall-to-wall marble, so that's really my only negative, but it's not overwhelming.
Great food, terrible service.  A bit Pretentious. There are better options in and near the area.

Food was amazing, as was the service.  I would have given it 4 stars, however, I likely will not dine there again because they snuck a 5% surcharge for "restaurant operating costs".  This was not disclosed in advance and I did not notice it until I got home. This means the gratuity I left was based calculated on the inclusion of the surcharge.  Not right!

Outrageous quality and the only place in ca that has Paris quality croissants.  Had the lobster hash and blew me away.  There's an actual lobster tail in it.  They have a nice $15 valet out front due to limited parking.

It was the type of day where I just let my wife pick where we ate, so for lunch I ended up at [REDACTED]. We've been wanting to check this place out for a while and her hankering for Italian food (which is pretty much every meal anyways) took us here. Now, I realize I probably made a weird ordering choice but thanks to us coming for brunch and not really being in the mood for some pizza, I ended up ordering the Cheeseburger and Fries and it was what it was. The burger tasted like a good burger and the fries tasted good, but it was a pretty standard experience and one I probably wouldn't make again. Since I jumped straight to the food there, let's rewind. This place is absolutely massive and located right in the heart of downtown. Not only is this a full restaurant, but there's also a bakery up front with a ton of different sweet treats as well. We showed up around noon on a Sunday and were able to sit down right away. The staff here was very kind and pretty quick throughout our visit, filling glasses and taking orders pretty quickly. The whole experience front to back was very pleasant and I have absolutely nothing negative to say.

Overall, [REDACTED] was a great place to grab lunch and a place I look forward to coming back to for some pizza in the future. The real takeaway? I really can't wait to come back and try some baked goods in the future! Gimme sweets, ya know.
Super good pasta and pizza at an affordable price , i wouldn't say to go dressed up super fancy but it is pretty nice on the inside more of a casual wear the gnocchi pasta is amazing

It's been awhile since my last visit. As always the food is consistently delicious. Service this visit was a little slow but we enjoyed ourselves very much once the food arrived. We split the club sandwich and it was loaded with deliciousness. My frisée salad with chicken was very delicious.Love the whole vibe here and the bakery area is just so beautiful. Parked across the street $15.00 for 2 hours . Always so excited to bring home some patisserie's and macarons for the family.

businessVideos.caption: The macaron display is so beautiful!

text.full: Wonderful vibe in here, no need to get too deep into it because the pics do it justice (for a change; many places are hard to get the feel of from pics, this one is so open and sun-welcoming that it is how it looks in the pics lol). You will enjoy every single salad if you're fond of salads without too heavily flavored dressings (crab [REDACTED] is a good example as some ppl I've dined with wanted more oomph w the dressing, i find it perfect because it doesn't overpower the star of the salad). Pizzas are delicious, solid ingredients and more than reasonable size. Only reason for the 4 star frankly is the fact I like other pizza spots better and it's not letting me add 4.5‍. For what it's worth, [REDACTED], you guys are NO LESS than four and a half and the vast majority of your staff are wonderful people. It is clear you train staff well and as a former service industry worker I respect that wholeheartedly.

If you're looking for pastries, look no further. [REDACTED] has an impressive selection in a gorgeous space. 

For Italian food, Los Angeles has many more impressive options. The food is by no means bad, but service is standoffish, the space is loud, and food is overpriced and uninspired. Especially downtown, [REDACTED] struggles to compete against Italian options like Maccheroni Republic or the many other excellent restaurants steps away.

Wow! Jennifer was absolutely amazing at serving our every need. She was very attentive while being timely and assisting our every need.  A must get is the vodka pizza, it was great! Thank you Jennifer !

Food: 3
Service: 3
Ambiance: 4

[REDACTED] is undeniably beautiful -- bright, airy, aesthetic, and just a really fun place to walk into. It has that iconic LA vibe that makes it perfect for bringing out-of-town visitors who want something memorable and visually stunning. I had a friend visiting from out of the country and staying nearby, so I wanted to take her somewhere special for brunch. I'd heard a lot about [REDACTED] and was excited to try it for the first time. We went on a Sunday morning during brunch and I made reservations expecting it to be packed, but it actually wasn't too busy. There were people, but it wasn't overwhelming at all. Parking is mostly street parking, so definitely give yourself some extra time to find a spot. They also have valet parking available, which is convenient if you don't want to circle the blocks. The service at the front was just okay -- not rude, but not particularly welcoming either. Once seated, the service was fine but nothing memorable. For brunch, we ordered the waffles, avocado toast, a burger, salmon benedict and a matcha latte. Honestly, everything was just okay. Nothing was bad, but nothing really stood out flavor-wise. I was honestly a bit underwhelmed. We didn't try the desserts this time since we had plans to go somewhere else afterward, but the dessert counter looked gorgeous. Overall, the ambience and aesthetic definitely outshine the food and service. I'm glad I tried it once, especially to take my visiting friend somewhere pretty and iconic, but it's probably not a place I'd return to.

Holy moly what a fun experience! Don't get distracted by the beautiful pastries on your way into the restaurant for dinner! (We almost did.) Definitely check Yelp/resy to make an easy reservation first - we saw some people get turned away when we arrived because they didn't have a reservation. 

Our eyes are definitely bigger than our stomachs. Start off with a beautiful cocktail. Might I strongly suggest the pear martini? It'll sneak up on you, because it's so delicious. I'm a sucker for arancini's and a Caesar, and both  didn't disappoint. (If you're not into the deconstructed salad thing, beware.) Our mains are pasta. Cacio e Pepe - so yum!!! And if you got the time and a sweet tooth, definitely try the chocolate souffle. It'll be piping hot when it arrives 30min after you order, but it is worth it! 

Then when you're on your way out, get all the pastries to go! Their boxes are beautiful!!
One of the best restaurants in downtown! I love their food here! Their pizza is so delicious and their desert and drinks are to die for!! Definitely coming back!

What an amazing experience! You walk into the most gorgeous bakery, set with in an old marble building with a beautiful open layout. 

We were seated right away and service was outstanding! The girls and I were starving after our night before with twenty one pilots! 

As all the plates came out we were eyeballing all the delicious food we saw coming out before ours. Top pick was Le pancake and the Madame Croquette!!! The salmon Benedict was great as well but would have loved an amazing fresh baked English muffins over the potatoes. 
After eating we went to the bakery to grab some snacks to take home and share. The chocolate croissant was outstanding. 
If you're in LA and nearby do go check out this spot. I will definitely be going back!

Huge space! They were waiting to open a section when we arrived, it was packed by the time we left. The food was amazing, I had a he bench pasta and my wife had eggs Benedict, so delicious. Drinks were on point too, Bloody Mary was very tasty.

[REDACTED] is always a treat! It's elegant, lively, and the food is absolutely delicious. My daughter and I love coming here--the atmosphere feels special every time, and everything from the pastries to the pastas is perfection. One of our favorite places in the city
Rafael is the best of the best just ask for him and the menu is the best. Everything is awesome here and make sure you ask for Rafayel amazing Customer service outstanding personality.

This is for their online service only. I made a mistake to order online to pickup macarons. Would have been a lot quicker if I had just ordered in person. My online was missed and the staff at the counter seemed clueless of which macarons to pick. Probably because the macarons list online didn't match with what were currently available. So their online service for the pastry counter was 1-star but the tastes of their macarons are still 5 stars to balance it out.

They were able to accommodate the food to make it vegan even if it's not on the menu. I had the pizza with no cheese, just red sauce with lots of veggies. I also had a custom pasta and the food was delicious!

Such a beautiful, luxe restuarant with great food and pretty pastries. I loved the bright and elegant decor/atmosphere - it made the experience really enjoyable. The open kitchen was fun, too. We got the sausage pizza and it was some of the best pizza we've ever had - thin with an ultra-crispy crust and the perfect ratio of sauce, cheese, and toppings. My cobb salad was really good, too! The complimentary bread was cute and fresh/warm - tasted a bit floury but it was good with the butter.

I liked their macarons, though pistachio tasted a tad artificial and earl grey was slightly too bitter. The plain croissant and pain au chocolat were just ok. The pain au chocolat barely had chocolate in it. The chocolate eclair (with a chocolate cream filling) was good, but on the drier/denser side. 

The atmosphere and dine-in food were the stars. The patries were gorgeous but nothing special taste-wise. Overall, I highly recommend - this was worth the trip!

This is one of my go-tos when I'm in downtown LA! Lots of space for dining, which typically means short waits. Fun layout so you can see the food being made and the dessert you can purchase. After discovering the Trenne pasta, I crave it every time I'm in the area! Fast service and complementary bread, it's a great experience!

This review is for the pastries and desserts,  they're a must! The pastries are delicious, and the macarons are incredible. Their cakes are beautiful too, I've gifted them before, and they're always a hit. Service is attentive. The presentation is beautiful and elegant. I still need to try the restaurant itself (it's a bit loud though definitely not a date spot).

Amazing food and even better desserts! Every dish was packed with flavor and beautifully presented. The vibe is classy yet relaxed, and the service was top-notch. Definitely one of my new favorite spots in Downtown LA -- can't wait to go back!

I feel like I just walked into heaven. The venue was open and the decor absolutely beautiful. I only went for the macaroons and they are like none other. I see why they are all the rave. Be warned if you go your regular macaroons will never taste the same!! So airy, so flavorful. Definitely would go back if in the area.
Great atmosphere, wonderful servers and delicious food.  I reminded me of a restaurant in New York City.

Go here every chance you get! A smidge pricey but the food is delicious!

Any of their patisserie are delicious. Macarons, I honestly thought would be better but okay. 

The pizza is delicious. Ordered carbonara and WOW it was so good with the bucatini pasta. Reasonable portion size that was just enough of such a rich pasta dish. Also ordered croque madame and damnnnnn it was huge. More than enough for two people or one very very hungry person. 

I come here every time I am in LA. You should too!!!!

I went here with a friend who knows I love Italian and the service at this eatery was absolutely divine. Our service was the best in the business and the cuisine was like nothing I ever had. Great job! And the decor was so modern, I felt really comfortable here.

This is my favorite place ever -- hands down! I love their French macarons; my ultimate favorite is Earl Grey! 
I usually go to the Santa Monica location, but I was in the area and decided to visit this one too. Not disappointed! The service is always impeccable  Bravo! I definitely highly recommend it -- 5 stars on their patisserie!

A timeless Los Angeles institution still setting the standard for excellence.

Andre Miripolsky and I had dinner last night at [REDACTED] in downtown Los Angeles, and it reminded me why this restaurant remains one of the cornerstones of LA's culinary identity. From the moment we arrived, the service was exceptional. I started with a perfectly balanced dirty martini at the bar -- cold, crisp, and beautifully prepared.

Andre ordered the chef salad and immediately said it was the best he's ever had. I had the braised beef, which ranks among the best dishes I've had anywhere, period. Every detail, from the preparation to the presentation, reflected the same precision and consistency that made [REDACTED] a downtown landmark years ago.

Having grown up around fine dining and spent years working in Michelin-level environments before graduate school and law, I can say without hesitation that what they've achieved here deserves recognition. This place has helped define downtown Los Angeles as an international destination, and it continues to perform at the highest level.

Thank you Ivan, you did a wonderful job. 

Highly recommended.

Viva LA and Fear No Art

businessPhotos.caption: Bottega Louie located at grand and 7th St. Downtown Los Angeles.

businessVideos.caption: Andre and I present two masterpieces from Bottega Louie -- the chef salad and braised beef, both absolute perfection.

text.full: Came here with my husband for an early dinner before attending a concert at Walt Disney Concert Hall on a Sunday. The place was not busy at all and there were many tables available. We ordered the Caesar salad, Margarita pizza and lobster bisque. The pizza was very good, the salad was ok also. We absolutely loved the lobster bisque; so delicious! They being warm bread with butter to start. Would definitely go back to Rey something else. We don't really understand the 5% surcharge. We think maybe restaurants should increase prices slightly instead of having this fee.

I went to this place because my fiancé said it was good. After my visit, I must agree. The atmosphere was cool, the appetizers (small plates) were awesome, our entrees were delicious, and the drink I had (Pear Martini) was delicious. Our server, Alex, was a really good guy. Very attentive, very personable, and knowledgeable. If you are downtown LA, want some good food. This is one place I would recommend. I heard they have really good pizza as well. I was impressed by my entree, I ordered a pizza to go. It is also delicious

This restaurant has the best fresh pasta dishes and bakery items.  They are known most for their delectable macaroons which I bought a box of to share with my coworkers.  The layout is open concept so you can see all the chefs hard at work in your skillfully crafted and delicious meals.  The staff were friendly, attentive and the wait for our food was not too long.  We took a Waymo to this location which is in downtown Los Angeles. Waymos don't have much spacial awareness in busy downtown areas yet so the drop off was a bit awkward given there was not much room to be dropped off right on the curb.  A ride share driven by a human may be able to drop you off more gracefully.  However you arrive,  you are sure to enjoy what you decide to order at this business.  Highly recommend a stop if you are in town.

businessPhotos.caption: To order a box of macaroons you'll select the ones you want in this list.
We were seated right next to the wood fire oven where they made the pizzas fresh.
Bread at the table and fresh butter

text.full: I've been back here a couple of times and my experiences have actually gotten really better. The drinks were really good and the food was delicious. So was the service very quick and nice. Super cute spot.
Good food, poor service from the front desk. She was very rude but the waiters were kind. Ambiance was warm, a little loud though.

Ambiance is great, large, spacious. Quiet enough for conversation but just enough buzz
Service is formal and kind
Food:
Carbonara - sooo decadent and delicious 
[REDACTED] salad - I am literally craving this salad. Refreshing, light, scrumptious. Seriously something that I will be thinking about for a while now
Portobello fries - kind of overrated in my opinion, would pass next time

This Italian restaurant and bakery has been around for decades. Hubby's work is nearby so he's passed by this place countless times. We decided to come in for brunch on a Saturday. Place is very spacious and open with a little bakery cafe in the front. 

The brunch menu was bigger than thought. We decided on portobello fries, calamari, steak & eggs and lobster hash. Also sparking water, oat milk matcha latte and double espresso as beverages. The matcha latte was so rich and strong which was exactly how I liked it. The food was so delicious in big portions, I especially loved the portobello fries. Service was friendly and attentive enough for a casual brunch. 

We would definitely come back for dinner soon, I really want to try the pizza and pasta dishes! We got a box of assorted macron to go and they tasted great! I will get some other desserts to go next time cause they looked so yummy!

I've been dining at [REDACTED] for 13 years, and somehow it never disappoints. Every visit still feels like a first love. 

The desserts are as stunning as ever--tiny works of art that taste as good as they look. The pasta with jumbo shrimp was delicious (originally it's with lobster, but our waiter kindly swapped it since they were out that day), and heirloom burrata salad were perfect. Even the simple Epi bread with butter, served right on the paper-lined table, feels effortlessly chic.

Funny thing: for some reason, I always end up here with old friends I haven't seen in years--and every single time, we take a photo in front of the dessert display. Same spot, same smiles, different year. Maybe it's fate... or just really good lighting.

After all these years, [REDACTED] still feels like the heart of downtown--beautiful, delicious, and timeless.
Drinks were good, pasta was good too. But I do have to agree with some of the other reviews, the prices do not match the quality. I think it could be a bit cheaper.

My friend from Boston took me here for a birthday lunch, for starters, the pasta I ordered was good. However, the beverages were sub-par and the appetizer/bread was okay. It was over-hyped, to be frank, the prices did not match the lack of quality overall. And the pizza was more a flatbread. I would give them another chance during dinner. The indoor decor was very bourgeois and felt like an old bank turned into a macaroon pastry restaurant. Our server from LA was very nice.

Walked in and seated right away. Our server took our order within minutes......about 30 mins of waiting, she comes to say that their wifi went down and had to be re-set, she wanted to verify our order. The lady who came way after we did received her order in minutes and the family of 6 who came in after 30 mins of waiting received their dinner! So disappointed! Very poor service! Not coming back!
The breakfast was amazing!! The service was top notch. I highly recommend this place.

Our group of three individuals enjoyed the atmosphere and the food very much.  The service was also great.  I loved the way they serve the warm and delicious, as well as pretty presentation of the  bread directly on the paper lining the table.  The noise level however, was awful.  The high ceilings and hard surfaces contributed the a VERY LOUD environment.  It made it extremely difficult to hear the person next to you in normal conversation.  Not sure if the owners are willing to try to combat that issue but I would be more inclined to go back if it wasn't so darn loud.

The restaurant is really pretty inside. I remember it used to always be packed. I think it's a cute spot for a first date. The food is easy to eat, good, though not the most delicious, and reminds me a bit of an upscale Cheesecake Factory. The waitress was working really hard. The meatballs were my least favorite dish.

This place is definitely overhyped and overpriced. Especially there being so many other bakeries with better service, quality and pricing. I'd rather go to Susie's cakes or bakers bench in Chinatown if you're vegan.

Prior to visiting La we did some research on the best rated restaurants by our hotel and stumbled across [REDACTED]. We were sold and made a reservation for dinner. Once we arrived at [REDACTED] we were immediately sat even though we were early. The restaurant itself is very open and also has a nice bar area. I liked that they had two different kitchens that you could see into. 

The Pizza Burrata had prosciutto slices and burrata cheese on top. A great combo of creamy and salty with a very nice wood fire oven cooked dough. The pizza had great crisp and crust. The chicken Parmesan was incredible. Huge portion of chicken nice crust and delicious fresh sauce. We ordered a side of homemade pasta to go with it which was extremely fresh and delicious. Overall our dining experience was great and will be back next time we are in the area.

3 Starts for the restaurant mains.  I now understand why the Yelp "Popular dishes" show only pizzas and desserts, because these are their best items, along with some appetizers (the arancini fried risotto balls are really good.)  
My friend says his grilled branzino was just Ok.
My 16oz ribeye medium rare was overcooked, with lots of fat and tough sinew in almost every bite.  Should probably have sent it back but it was late (9:40p) after LA Opera and I was starving and did not want to wait another 25 minutes for a new entree.

Absolutely love the place. Pizza, pasta, salad, and chicken parm were fantastic. We weren't too crazy about the ribeye and portobello fries through. Very friendly staff and great ambiance. Based on the location and decor, I expected the prices to be somewhat higher, but it was more than reasonable (all things considering). Being in DTLA, parking will always be a problem. We parked valet ($15 + tip). Next time, I may try taking the Metro here for brunch.
Food was great, obsessed with the desserts. Had no idea this place was hiding downtown, it felt like you were somewhere else when inside. Definitely will come back!

Always have a great time here on anyway of the week. I typically come here alone to do remote work or just enjoy some TLC.

Ambiance is very open, aesthetic, and  beautiful.

I primary sit at the bar by default, you can't go wrong sitting anywhere honestly. 

During my latest visit on a Tuesday around 1pm, I ordered the Portobello fries, Lobster Bisque asked for the cream on the side (ridiculously good) & an expresso Martini.

It was very relaxed, with mild capacity of foot traffic.

My server at the bar was absolutely amazing, I wish I remembered his name! Enjoy!

businessPhotos.caption: Lobster Bique, Portobello Fries & Expresso Martini.

text.full:
Came here for a cake, we got the La Peche large size. Surprisingly it was not too sweet and the tart itself was very well received by the older asian crowd! The custard was very light and creamy smooth, size-wise easily served 9 adults and have some to spare. 

The peach slices itself were delicious, pretty thin, but some of the slices were definitely starting to turn dark by the end of the day

My husband and I had a deeply disappointing experience at [REDACTED]. We were initially seated next to the restrooms. When I requested a window seat, I was told those servers were "too busy" and offered a table by the kitchen instead. Minutes later, a white-presenting man and his companion were moved to the exact window section we were denied.
When I questioned the maître d', she attempted to gaslight me by claiming she would have accommodated us anywhere--a clear contradiction. My husband raised our concerns with the manager, only to be met with dismissiveness and a token offer of dessert.
As loyal patrons who have recommended this restaurant widely, we left feeling excluded, unwelcome, and profoundly disheartened. This experience exposed a troubling lack of inclusivity and accountability at [REDACTED].

businessPhotos.caption: pizza Box with the business name

text.full: I would like to start off by saying I did not stay here and eat or sit at the bar. I come here for coffee and pastries occasionally after leaving Whole Foods. The food does look really good and I would love to stay one day. I usually get an iced mocha. The service is really fast. You just make an order at a kiosk and pay there as well. You can order pastries there or go up to the workers in front of the pastries. There is never a line for this. Valet parking is an option or there are parking garages near. This place is beautiful, Very clean and aesthetic. The packaging is also gorgeous so it is perfect as a gift. There are a ton of pastry options!

Taste of the dessert is just okay, not matching the price. The worst part is the staff serving at the dessert counter. When I tried to order, the male servant just skipped me and said he had to offer service to another man who is coming after me. Excuse me?? Thank God, another male servant came to me and helped me to check out. But I was still so disappointed after I opened the box, because they used a much larger size box to package a very small slice of cake, making the cake finally just look like trash.  Good thing I didn't intend to purchase them for gifts. They need further training to become professional staff. Totally not worth it.

I finally made it to [REDACTED] in downtown LA, and it's every bit as beautiful (and bustling) as people say. The space feels like stepping into a European patisserie--marble counters, glass cases of jewel-toned macarons, and the buzz of happy diners.

I went for brunch and ordered the eggs benedict and a cappuccino. The presentation was on point, and everything tasted fresh. The portions are generous without being over the top. 

The service was friendly but expect a bit of a wait, especially on weekends; it's that popular. Prices are a little on the higher side, but you're paying for the atmosphere and quality.

Overall, [REDACTED] delivers on the hype--a perfect spot if you're in LA and want a chic brunch or patisserie experience.
The baked sweet confections are in a class by themselves!  The selection, quality, and presentation is fantastic!

Ivan, Ivan, Ivan... one of the best servers we've had in a while. He was friendly, patient with us, and we never felt like he was rushing us. He had great recommendations off of the menu. 

The food was great, from the Tartufo pizza, to the calamari, to the [REDACTED] salad, everything was amazing. Would strongly recommend anyone to come to [REDACTED]!

My wife and I go here all the time for brunch, dinner and pastries! We also bring our guests from out of town here as well and they love it too!  Everything is excellent, vibe, food, coffee and service. Feels like eating in Italy. We love this place! We're actually going tonight and I can't wait! Must go place in LA. William, our server, last time did an excellent job. He was very friendly, patient and funny.

Lovely building with some French cafe vibes but on a grand and modern scale   

Allegedly this place used to be better but I think it was pretty damn good!  We started with an arugula salad which was nice and 'spicy'   Crispy shallots were surprising but good. Could have used some more lemon. 

Got a loaf of bread smacked onto the table. Nice interior. The butter was hard and I wish it was soft. Also needed salt but they do have himilayan salt in the shakers so that was good 

Pasta was the carbonara which was served with a beautiful 32* egg. It was gorgeous and decadent and 10/10. Could come back just for this. 

They're known for their pizza   And I see why. Naples style, it's thin and crispy with a **perfect** crust. Not an ounce of grease to be seen and the middle of the pizza held up which is rare I feel like with the stone ovens. 

Overall, it's a yes from me.
Ambience was great. Service very slow and main salads both missing dressings... worst of all, 5% ' surcharge" towards operating costs.. such nonsense.. will never go back
Food was delicious, but the desserts were the best part for me. I definitely might have had more than delectable treat during my stay in LA.

Dropped by BL, one of our favorite DTLA spots before a Dodger game earlier this year. Ordered a pizza and a glass of house chardonnay. When done, the bill for the wine was $36. I asked the server why she didn't advise us, as that it almost twice the price of a house wine anywhere else. She simply said that was the price.
I wrote to the restaurant's principal, complaining, and never received a response. Disgusting and beyond disappointing. Not returning, after enjoying for 15 years or so.

I made a yelp account just to leave a review I would leave 1 star but it gets 2 because the service was great I went yesterday with my family to celebrate my sisters 25th birthday the food was ok thats it nothing crazy not only was it ok I got FOOD poisoning from the lobster hash I had ordered and it couldn't have been anything else because that was my FIRST meal of the day and it couldn't have been the pizza we ordered as well because I was the ONLY one who felt sick out of my whole family couple hours after eating from there I had the biggest urge to throw up I felt HORRIBLE this was my first AND MY LAST time coming here I can't believe I spent 35 dollars just to get food poisoning

[REDACTED] had gone down the hill. half of the dessert section is empty, waiters are not so nice ( We asked for bread. (10 min after asking ) she replied "I have not left this section" i know she's overloaded with work. she looked stressed out. mind you we got there before rush hour it was not busy yet. Anyhow, my pasta was burnt and crispy who crisps pasta did not taste good. waiter again said you should have mentioned something, she was no where to be found.  Last complain... no where in the restaurant does it say 5% surcharge... a surprise change on top of our over charged meals...

businessPhotos.caption: surcharges, no where advertised just a nice surprise when check out!

text.full: Came here for brunch with the family during a long weekend getaway and found this restaurant with delicious food and yummy macarons.  Being that my kids loved macarons, we decided to give it a try.  Turned out the only thing better than the food was the service.  The staff is great and we want to give a special thanks to Yahaira (our host) @ [REDACTED] for the wonderful experience.  We got seated a little later than expected and she felt bad and even checked with her manager to ensure we were taken care of during our time at the restaurant.  She is wonderful and we're so thankful and appreciative of her, her manager, and the entire staff.  Thank you so much for the attentiveness and checking up on us.  We'll definitely be back and really wanted to give her and the restaurant a big thumbs-up!  Thank you!!!
Drinks, pizza, steak & eggs were all delicious... 10/10 recommend.  Our waiter Ivan was on point with all his recommendations, he definitely has good taste.

I ordered 60 chocolate covered sunflower seeds. That's $600. My first order with them was for 90 - $900. I use them as client gifts. I placed the order on Wednesday and confirmed with them that I would be there Saturday to pick it up. When I got there it wasn't ready and the manager said she had to go pick it up and she would be "right back." I waited for 45 minutes. Apparently she had to walk down the street somewhere to go get the order. I told them politely that I was in a hurry and had to be somewhere. That is why I planned ahead for it to be ready. They made me late to my next stop. I really wasn't going to give them a bad rating, things happen but when she finally got back she was laughing with another employee and paid no attention to the 45 minutes she had been gone. No sorry. No nothing. I've spent over $1500.00 there on these overpriced fancy gifts and now I'm done. My time is valuable too.

8.28.2025

I returned back because I suggested to my co worker to get a cake here. She lives in San Diego so this was all new to her. She thought only 85 degrees was the only bakery that had cake. I said oh no. We need a fancy cake for our fellow Co worker.

I'm going to change my rating soley on how beautiful it is inside. She was amazed by how beautiful it was. Even for myself, I had to take more pictures. Every piece of cake/tart was immaculate.

I can't give a five star just yet. The ladies at the counter, do they not know how to smile or greet customers? I think it's a lost cause.

My co worker purchased 2 beignets and a full size La Pêche. Pictures do not do these pastries justice.

Me and my family just went here last Friday and the food was amazing for starters we had a crab cake it was so good I was at a loss for words then we had a pepperoni pizza. OH MY GOSH it is one of the best pizzas I have ever tasted in my life. The service was fast and efficient. The ok of the restaurant was just amazing it had a very classy look to but also nice and comfortable. I would definitely recommend this restaurant it is one of the best in Los Ang

[REDACTED] Louis is one of my frequently visited places on a weekly basis.I always go there with for a weekend brunch  as both of us have plenty of time to spend outside on weekends.We love their food,ambience,and vibe.A wonderful restaurent to visit  anytime for any type of appetite!

This restaurant has been here for a long time, and I decided to check it out again after last having been here over 10 years ago. My bf and I came here for brunch and got seated right away despite not having reservations during what seemed like rush hour for the restaurant. 

Food (5/5) - We ordered the lobster hash and the smoked salmon benedict. I really liked the lobster hash, it was flavorful and savory and filling. I wish the hash was separated from the sauce as I don't like soggy food, but to my surprise, it was still good and a bit crispy even after being soaked in the sauce. The lobster piece was big and the sauce was creamy and thick. My bf ordered the smoked salmon benedict and also liked it, saying that the smoked salmon was nice and not too fishy. 

Service (5/5) - The service was good, the staff friendly. We ordered and the food came shortly after. They refilled our waters throughout the meal and were attentive when we tried to call them for the bill. 

Ambiance (5/5) - The restaurant is big, clean, bright and bustling. There's ample seating indoors, with big windows, marble flooring, and a patisserie that's known for their macaroons. The prices are pretty high here, but overall it's a nice place to check out every once in a while. It's located in downtown LA but they have other locations as well. We parked in a paid lots a block away from this restaurant.
If you've ever been, I highly recommend for breakfast on the weekend. I usually order their pancakes. 12/10 if you love a sweet tooth.

I went to [REDACTED] in Downtown LA for a family dinner on my birthday last year in November, and then again with my bestie on August 2nd for dinner.

I've been to this place several times over the past 10 years, and I really like it. Their Carbonara Spaghetti is absolutely delicious, and I also enjoy the Pomodoro. The pizza, baked in a wood-fired oven, is especially amazing.

However, one dish to avoid here is the Meatball Spaghetti--it was just meh and not worth it. Other than that, the food is great, the service is good, and the ambience is really nice. Their macarons and cakes are also excellent.

I also visited [REDACTED] in West Hollywood about two years ago, but since it's been a while, it's a bit hard to find those photos. Maybe I'll post them later if I get a chance.

One thing I wish they would do, though, is inform customers in advance about the fees they add to the receipt. It would be better if they mentioned it upfront.

Love there beignets are so delicious. One of the best pastas carbonara is very creamy first time trying it and it blow my mind having it with a poached egg was like the cherry on top. Must try also tuna tartare best appetizers. Service was great
Jeff was very attentive and kind! Great service!!!!! Will come back again just for Jeff and the food of course!

Marble floors, pastel macarons, and a buzzing café vibe. The food is next-level: fluffy waffles, truffle arancini, and thin-crust pizzas that actually taste as good as they look. Service is quick and friendly, and yes, the pastry display is totally Instagram-worthy. Perfect spot for brunch, dessert, or just soaking in that LA-meets-Paris energy.

Ive never been to Botegga Loyie6but it was my daughter's birthday and they were open early on a Sunday. Lucky me, parking ride outside the door and they had a a plethora of macarons which are my daughters favorite. 

I gave them to my daughter ina beautiful wrapped gift box and she loves them.

Ill be back they have some other items I'd like to try out.

businessPhotos.caption: Cute little bakers dozen box of macarons.

text.full: Naturally, the food is bomb AF but that A+ service from KEVIN is what keeps me coming back. If y'all dine here, make sure to bee seated in KEVIN's SECTION! His attention to detail, hospitality & he truly cares about the experience he's creating! Well done!

We had a large part and all of the food was amazing. Everyone at the table of 14 was raving about their meals. The service was excellent - we felt well taken care of the entire evening. The ambiance is a little lacking as the space is cavernous with nothing to absorb the sound so it's a little loud. All in all, it was a great evening and as long as you're okay with some noise, it's a nice place for dinner.

Go here immediately. Seriously, food is delicious, service is exceptional, space is beautiful! The hostesses were friendly and kind, our waitress was WONDERFUL! So attentive! We loved the bolognese and pizza and the calamari! The macaroons are so incredible too! I really cannot say enough good things about our visit. Everything works perfectly here, the food is absolutely delicious and fresh, the employees and manager were so lovely and professional and I think everyone needs to be eating here because this was hands down our best dining experience ever. Kudos to all the amazing staff and people running this establishment! We will return soon and thank you endlessly for such a beautiful dinner!

So glad that [REDACTED] now takes reservations! When I last visited a few years ago, they did not. I recently introduced my partner to this resaurant, and with the reservation, we did not have to wait at all. 

I decided to try the margherita pizza, which has mozzarella, pecorino romano, tomato sauce, fresh basil, and oregano. It was excellent. The slices were nicely thin and crispy. The portobello fries are a MUST. I would order them every time I come here. 

There is a patisserie section with a bunch of sweets and pastries. I cannot help but grab a few things here since the presentation is spectacular. There are many flavors of macarons. I tried the earl grey, vanilla, espresso, and pistachio, all of which I enjoyed.

My partner and I had a reservation for this restaurant and as soon as we walked in, we were greeted with the smell of desserts and snacks. The style of the restaurant is immaculate as there's a golden/white sparkling when entering, similar to marble type environment, super modern and well designed. 

Parking is a bit limited, no designated structure or lot but plenty of paid parking lots across/nearby to accommodate, not too expensive. 

Since we had a reservation, we were seated super quick even though it was quite busy. Service was great, our server checked up on us on our order and refills very often along with ordering speed. 

We ordered a margarita pizza, omelette, and a "must try" Portobello Fries, all shareable dishes. Pizza was bigger than expected but it was super delicious with a nice crunchy crust. Omelette is well cooked and has good portions. Although I feel like there was too much veggies and less of the omelette, nonetheless, still pretty yummy. Lastly, the Portobello Fries were an absolute treat, as someone who loves mushrooms and fries, this was a perfect combo. Highly recommend even for non mushroom lovers. Only downside to the fries is the container that it comes in, although great aesthetic, the pieces that fall off can easily make a mess on the table, not a huge downside but one to look at. 

After the meal, we took a look at the bakery and we were enamored with the amount of macaron choices, a huge variety and super affordable. There were plenty of other desserts but we were too full to continue! We will definitely be back to try other menu items and bakery catalogs. Food was amazing, dessert is plentiful, service is great, and environment is perfect for a date setting.

businessPhotos.caption: Pistachio, expresso, earl grey, and vanilla macarons

text.full:
I've been coming here since I was a little girl, I come here with my mom sometimes. 
This is one of our favorite spots to eat at when it's a special occasion. The food is always amazing, the waiters and staff always have a great humbling attitude and the environment is pretty good too, inside the restaurant it kinda sounds like you're at a loud party because of all the conversations everyone is having with one another at their tables lol

I am so happy that I had the opportunity to come back to [REDACTED]! I absolutely love this restaurant. It is aesthetically pleasing with all the different colored macarons and then the delicious pastries and baked goods. 

I love the open kitchen concepts too. You can view in the bakery, the kitchen and the pizza area too. The space here is sprawling! I wish there was an option for an enclosed patio, but it's a city, so I know that's really wishful thinking. 

Our food was delicious - especially the French toast! I also got a couple cookies to go and the oatmeal raisin was delicious. I think it was caramelized and it was amazing! 

Everyone needs to come to [REDACTED] at least once! You won't be disappointed!

Steak was mid asf for the price (65) was expecting a nice cut of steak 
Service was very good always checking up on us
Place felt sad

Whenever there's a special event, I always come here to buy desserts and cakes! They're always delicious, and I'm always satisfied with the new seasonal menus too. It's a place I'd recommend to anyone. I've also had dinner here before, and I was surprised at how high-quality the food was not just the desserts. Whether it's for an anniversary dinner or just for dessert and cake, this is definitely a restaurant I'd love to recommend.
