Not a lot of choice and can't give small spoons to eat dessert, only forks . Would not recommend

Been here only for dinner on a few occasions so first time for breakfast and didn't disappoint. Napolean was a good server. I ordered croque Madame I'm not sure what made it a Madame vs Monsieur iykyk. I didn't realize the egg was going to come on top of the sandwich and wasn't asked how I wanted the egg cooked, I don't care for the whites to be runny. The only downside is that, yes, very pricey cause no sides come with some of the options. Vanilla latte was ok, on the small side, drank that up quickly. My niece had a matcha latte and she loved it and her goat cheese omelette was good too. Her bf got the eggs any style with sausage, potatoes looked good and he was satisfied.

Everything was delicious!! 7/26/25

Portobello Fries ($18)
The fries are big wedge-like pieces with some cheese/parmesan and come with aioli sauce. The sauce could use more garlic but it was still good. The fries were crispy outside and soft inside. 

[REDACTED] Salad ($25)
Lots of flavor and the crab was fresh. Big portion! Good for splitting with 2-3 people if you split other dishes.

Hamburger ($25)
Medium well done burger was perfect and still very juicy. It's a nice thick patty. The bun got soggy already though. Huge portion and LOTS of fries. They were nice and crispy but not too stiff. Comes with three sauces (ketchup, some kind of spicy mayo?, and mustard) and pickles.

Pistachio Gelato ($12)
HUGE scoop and no artificial colors! The flavor is perfect and not too sweet. Comes with a crunchy sweet thing which I didn't like because it's too hard and sticks to my teeth. Doesn't break down easily.

Yuzu Calamansi Eclair ($8)
I personally don't really like eclairs because they're a bit hard and dry. But I love the strong yuzu flavor of this. Nice and citrusy.

It's pricey but a nice treat once in a while.

Street parking would be the best. $2 per hour with 4 hours max on some streets on Saturdays.

The server was very friendly.

It was very loud and hard to talk, and the AC was on very strong inside that day. The ambiance is nice though.

They have tons of pasty to choose from and coffee and tea ordering section too.

I often come here for desserts -- especially the mini pastries and tartlets. Everything is fresh, beautifully presented, and the flavors are always on point -- delicate and not overly sweet, just the way I like it.

That said, the prices are definitely on the higher side. A couple of small pastries can add up quickly, especially when they're boxed. I get that you're paying for the quality, presentation, and overall experience, but still -- it feels a bit pricey for what you get.

Still, it's a lovely treat once in a while. Perfect spot when you want something a little extra special, just not an everyday indulgence.

[REDACTED] is definitely a beautiful spot -- everything looks so elegant.
I got two pastries to go, and they were tasty, no doubt. The packaging is absolutely gorgeous -- their boxes look like little gifts!

But honestly, I feel the prices are on the higher side. You're definitely paying for the presentation and brand.

Worth trying if you want to treat yourself or grab something fancy, but I wouldn't go often just because of the cost.

My mom overheard the table next to us raving about the truffle pizza. We started chatting to her and decided we'd get it too. The pizza was exceptional. The crust was so fresh---you can even see it being made from your table. We paired it with the [REDACTED] salad, which was so fresh. Perfect pairing. Great service and great meal! Thank you Barbara for the recommendation and happy anniversary!
Very cute place! The decor reminds me of going to a nice lunch with friends. The food was pretty good and the service was too. Would go back with friends or on a cute outing with my sisters!
We ordered the short rib and pepperoni pizza for the teens with us. The rib was excellent and tasty, the boys said it was their best pizza ever.

I loved the Crème Brûlée donut--perfectly crisp on top and creamy inside, absolutely delicious. The Napoleon donut wasn't quite for me--too many competing flavors (sour, sweet, mango, lemon) that didn't blend well.

In the evening, we tried the truffle pizza, bolognese pasta, and Portobello mushroom fries--everything was absolutely delicious! The fries were crispy and packed with flavor, a perfect appetizer. 

The staff was super friendly, and the vibe of the place was warm and inviting. We had an amazing time and will definitely be back!

This is my favorite spot for brunch -- I've been coming here for 20 years, and it's always consistently good. Whenever I go out for brunch, this is the place I choose. I love the atmosphere, the interior decor, and the way the sunlight fills the space with energy.
Their desserts section still looking pretty nice. The macaroons were good and was a good price too. Will come back again for sure.

If you've completely stopped trying, just close your doors and call it a day.  Half of their pastry section was empty during lunch time and there were trash cans all over the floor as if there was a leak from the ceiling. Whatever we asked for, they didn't have any. They also told us that they discontinued the Napoleon, which used to be the best we ever had. The restaurant already went downhill years ago, so why even stay in business at this point if you don't care at all? We are officially done with this place.
Love the the food. really great quality food. love the presentation and the service they gave you. And the vibe is the best

Me and my family ordered some of the best food we have had in a while. I asked for medium rare steak and it was cooked to perfection ! Service was excellent , I felt like royalty to say the least. Overall I had a splendid  afternoon lunch that  I won't forget at [REDACTED].

My wife and I had dinner last night at [REDACTED] before a performance at the Ahmanson Theatre.
We had dined here previously. Large, beautiful, modern and just a bit loud.
We shared a Crab [REDACTED] salad and the chicken parmigiana. Both were excellent with large portions. For dessert we shared the beignets with raspberry sauce. Wonderful, like warm jelly filled donuts with a side raspberry sauce.
Our waiter, a very tall bald gentleman, was outstanding and deserves so much of the credit for our wonderful experience at [REDACTED]. We shall return!

Visit from 7/9 was a total disappointment. I used to dine at [REDACTED] regularly in the past. I have dined there over 40 times. It has always been one of my favorites. Since I had moved away from Southern California for a while I had not visited. I visited today 7/9 and was very disappointed with the food.

Our server repeatedly made mistakes. I asked for a Coke and was brought a Diet Coke. I asked for it to be corrected and once again he brought a Diet Coke and said , sorry here is your Diet Coke. 
My friend ordered a white wine and was brought out a rosè.

The pizza was a disaster. Not at all how I remember it. The Tartufo pizza is usually one of my favorites. It's usually a runny ( what I believe to be a poached egg ) and the yolk is drizzled all over the pizza.

This time it was disgusting. Literally a hard boiled egg as you can see in the photo. Solid yolk. It was my first time bringing my friend here and I was raving about the pizza. It was so embarrassing to have her be disgusted by a hard boiled egg on her pizza. 

The pizza was also burnt. Not the normal Char that can be expected. BURNT. I'm talking about you hit the crust with a knife and it makes a noise.

This was very embarrassing. I am very disappointed with my experience, [REDACTED] lost a loyal patron.

businessPhotos.caption: Disgusting hard boiled egg on the Tartufo pizza. Solid Yolk.

text.full:
Came by to celebrate a friend's birthday with brunch! 

So we dropped by around 10:00AM on a Sunday and there wasn't really that many people. The interior was very clean and bright, which I loved. They have their baked goods right when you walk in. You're able to order through their tablet on the side. 

My friends and I ordered a lot of the baked goods, I mainly got the croissants, but they all ordered a good amount. We shared a lot of them and two of my friends who are big on baked goods were super happy with what they got. 

We also got drinks! I loved my Arnold Palmer. It wasn't too sour and wasn't overly sweet. It was honestly perfect. 

The food was good. Nothing super amazing that blew my mind unfortunately. I probably should come by and try the pizza though. I got the Eggs Benedict, which was it should tastes like, runny egg yolks, ham, and spinach. I did get a different side though. Instead of their salads, I got potatoes. Unfortunately, some of them were undercooked. 

Service was alright too, though it was difficult to flag someone for help.

It was a good experience and I would probably give it another try soon!

Customer service is exclusive, the Food is amazing, the Desserts are a masterpiece, I've been coming come here since I Moved from Corona to Pico Rivera, I've been here over 100 times or maybe more! I've been here with parents and relatives from Guanajuato, MX. Fine dinning and fire pizzas and salads and non alcoholic drinks.

The food is definitely on the pricier side, but it's good nonetheless. Pastries are delicious and offer some unique flavors. Ambiance is lively, clean, and upscale--perfect for a trendy brunch spot. 

We got the Croque Madame, the Calzone Caprese, and the Avocado Toast. 

Docking a few points for the service. Our waitress was inattentive and the service was very slow. It made what I hoped would be a quick brunch take much longer than expected.

businessPhotos.caption: Avocado toast, Calzone caprese, Croque Madame

text.full:
I don't understand the great reviews about this place. The pasta was honestly terrible -- we both ordered different kinds and neither of us could finish them. 

The crunchy pasta???? had an odd texture -- somehow both soggy and overly firm in parts, like it had been overcooked, then left sitting too long.

As for the carbonara, it was honestly one of the worst we've had. It was greasy, bland, and missing that signature rich, creamy texture and flavor. It tasted more like an overly creamy butter cesspool than a proper carbonara. 

Even after taking the leftovers home, they still went untouched.

On the other hand, the soda was amazing -- super refreshing and flavorful, easily one of the best parts of the meal. Easily one of the BEST soda's I've ever had. That is unfortunately one of the only redeeming qualities of this meal. 

Dessert really stood out as well. We shared a large Macaron, which was not only beautifully presented but also delicious, with a rich creamy center and fresh blackberry.

The environment was okay -- stylish lighting and a vibrant crowd, though it was definitely too packed. The tables were placed a little too close together, which made things feel cramped. Still, it managed to be cozy and comfortable overall. Our server was quite attentive and nice, though it took a while to get seated. The hostess(?) seemed a little unfriendly. 

Between the great dessert and the atmosphere, there were definitely some positives, but the main courses left a lot to be desired. For a ~150$ meal, the quality was subpar. We will not be returning.
Foods great , pastries r even better . Very clean . It's in the heart of downtown la . Customer service is decent
Amazing pastries here (really... -wow!) AND the pizzas they make and serve here are *outstanding* all the way down to the last bite of crust (also wow!!). You pay for what you get here. Yummy!
Great date night for pizza and drinks inside an upscale environment. Great to stop by after an event in Dtla, visited for vantines with my valentine
the food quality is great but the ambiance is fantastic, the servers were amaazing, a bit of a while ago I got the pizza, it was absolutely delicious, the bakeries looked good but I didn't get any.
The food is divine, the service is really friendly. And the ambience inside is absolutely gorgeous. Highly recommend taking some Macarons for the road.

Came for brunch with my family. Food seems high quality and the pastries are gorgeous. The service was quick and friendly...I would definitely come back to try some other items on the menu. The Tartufo Pizza and Portobello fries were AMAZING. No complaints about the ambience but definitely feels like an upscale cafe.

businessPhotos.caption: Eggs any style, Belgian Waffles, Avocado Toast, Vodka Pizza, Brussels sprouts.

text.full:
FOOD: This is a great brunch spot in Downtown LA! Have been here multiple times with friends and the food is always really good! The Burrata Pizza is to die for! Their desserts and pastries are really good as well!! My favorite is their hazelnut beignets! I always get those when I go!

SERVICE: The service is always great. Servers and staff are always friendly!

AMBIANCE: Elegant and simple style! Very classy!

The restaurant itself is really nice with plenty of seating! I enjoyed the fried calamari, truffle pizza, and the açaí bowl. Their food was very delicious! They also have a desserts section at the front of the cafe which I always enjoy. I usually just stop by for just the desserts. Their cakes aren't too sweet which is perfect. There's a lot of people on weekends, so if you don't want to wait too long, I recommend visiting on weekdays.

Came back for lunch after 10 years and it's still good! Service was friendly and fast. The [REDACTED] crab meat salad tasted deliciously fresh. The complimentary bread was as hard as a rock, but it was free. 

The Iced Pistachio Latte from the cafe up front was also nice and creamy.

businessPhotos.caption: display case 1 of..6?  So many, I lost count.
Just the front 1/3 of the restaurant
Bottles of wine and olive oil, too
BLT and Lemon Ricotta Pancakes w/ housemade blueberry syrup

text.full: The establishment is very open with lots of seating. The entrance has a French bakery which is very unique. We didn't have a reservation, but we were seated quickly. The server was quick, and informative. It was our first time eating there so he made suggestions and could answer all questions we had. They serve complementary bread with butter, that's always a plus in my book. We started with meatballs, had vodka pizza, and ended with beignets. Everything was good.

My boyfriend was telling me about this place. It's very clean and big inside. Lots of macaroons and other pastries are here, but didn't get a chance to try. I tried their avocado toast which wasn't bad. I wanted to order others, but had an adventure of food that day so didn't want to over stuff myself. The waffle was crispy and fluffy. Overall great place to have a celebration or just a meal with a someone. Parking is street, but my hotel was nearby so that was a plus.

Had a great lunch here for my bday this week when my wife took me here! Not only was the food amazing, but the service was on point as well. 

Maddy (I think that's how you spell her name) was fantastic. Extremely efficient and attentive. We never had to flag her down for anything and she kept us taken care of. 

I had the salmon and it was sooooo bomb. Delicious and visually appealing. I forgot the name of the pasta my wife got but she loved it too. We also got some dessert to go before we left and I've had no impulse control on whipping it out every night lol

We were pleased to have discovered this spot, so close to our hotel. (the Biltmore). It is a huge open space, with a large number of tables and bench type seating, all very comfortable. Everything is a bit on the dark side, being white, but it certainly reflects cleanliness and precision.The only downside of the atmosphere (to me) was the height of the ceilings, which produce the typical and (by many) much beloved echoey feeling.

We started with mimosas (because, why not?) and followed up with a very nice salad (I can't remember which one, darn it) and my dish of poached eggs with lobster on a shredded potato base. I can't recall the sauce-- It was slightly bland but seemed to fit well with the overall dish. We had a small serving of bread (epi de ble) and finished with coffee, tea, and a very generous slice of raspberry chocolate torte. Overall, an absolutely wonderful lunch. Do not hesitate to visit this spot.

Stopped by for a quick drink at the bar. We were not greeted by any of the 3 bartenders just handed a menu. Not one came to ask how are drinks were. Took a walk to the pastry case, not greeted by any of the 4 employees just standing around and talking. Funny enough this restaurant was my client when I did PR. For a place that prides itself on service, it was severely lacking that day. Luckily it's cute in there.

Made a reservation for a weekday lunch and was seated immediately. Second time eating lunch at this location - acoustics make it very noisy so it's easier to have mules with small groups since large groups might not be able to hear each other well. Ordered the Portobello fries (very interesting & good aïoli), avocado, toast (kind of taste tasteless), carbonara (pretty good), club sandwich (meh.) Service was friendly, attentive and pretty quick.
